reasoning: The Bank Term Funding Program (BTFP) stopped making new loans on March 11, 2024. Because loans under the program had a maximum term of one year, all outstanding loans matured by March 11, 2025. The FRED data confirms this, with the series H41RESPPALDKNWW hitting exactly 0.000 on March 12, 2025 and remaining at 0.000 for over 17 weeks consecutively through late July 2025. Since the value on 2025-08-03 is 0.000, the only way for the series to increase is if the Federal Reserve officially reopens the BTFP and extends new loans. The program is definitively closed, and in the event of a future banking emergency, the Fed would likely utilize a new facility with a new FRED series ID rather than reopening this specific 2023 emergency program. Therefore, it is extremely unlikely (near the 0.02 minimum) that the value will increase. I have assigned a very slight increase for the longest horizons merely to reflect the theoretical tail risk of a black swan reopening event or accounting revision over an extended period.
